=== Quick Fix Inventory ===

[D1] (14:14-14:28) TS377047: This expression can be rewritten in the reusable function form `Effect.fn(() => { ... })`. effect(effectFnOpportunity)
  Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)"
  Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fn(\"arrowPlainMany\")"

[D2] (23:14-23:41) TS377047: This expression can be rewritten in the reusable function form `Effect.fn(function<T>(value) { ... })`. effect(effectFnOpportunity)
  Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)"
  Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fn(\"functionExpressionPlainMany\")"

[D3] (33:17-33:45) TS377047: This expression can be rewritten in the reusable function form `Effect.fn(function<T>(value) { ... })`. effect(effectFnOpportunity)
  Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)"
  Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fn(\"functionDeclarationPlainMany\")"

=== Quick Fix Application Results ===

=== [D1] Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D1] Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D1] Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_plainMany.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// There should be two code fixes offered:
// 1. Effect.fn("inferredName") - uses the inferred span name, improves
//    tracing by adding spans. Recommended for app code.
// 2. Effect.fn (without name) - won't add tracing spans.
//
// Note: Known formatting divergences from upstream (TypeScript-Go printer artifacts):
// - Semicolons added to body statements inside replaced functions (e.g. `const a = 1;` vs `const a = 1`)
// - Space before parens in zero-parameter function expressions (`function ()` vs `function()`)
// - Trailing semicolons on replacement statements (`});` vs `})`)

export const arrowPlainMany = Effect.fn(function () {
    const a = 1;
    const b = 2;
    const c = 3;
    const d = 4;
    const e = 5;
    return Effect.succeed(a + b + c + d + e);
});

export const functionExpressionPlainMany = function<T>(value: T) {
  const a = 1
  const b = 2
  const c = 3
  const d = 4
  const e = 5
  if (value === null) return Effect.fail("Error!")
  return Effect.succeed(a + b + c + d + e)
}

export function functionDeclarationPlainMany<T>(value: T) {
  const a = 1
  const b = 2
  const c = 3
  const d = 4
  const e = 5
  if (value === null) return Effect.fail("Error!")
  return Effect.succeed(a + b + c + d + e)
}


=== [D1] Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fn(\"arrowPlainMany\")" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_plainMany.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// There should be two code fixes offered:
// 1. Effect.fn("inferredName") - uses the inferred span name, improves
//    tracing by adding spans. Recommended for app code.
// 2. Effect.fn (without name) - won't add tracing spans.
//
// Note: Known formatting divergences from upstream (TypeScript-Go printer artifacts):
// - Semicolons added to body statements inside replaced functions (e.g. `const a = 1;` vs `const a = 1`)
// - Space before parens in zero-parameter function expressions (`function ()` vs `function()`)
// - Trailing semicolons on replacement statements (`});` vs `})`)

export const arrowPlainMany = Effect.fn("arrowPlainMany")(function () {
    const a = 1;
    const b = 2;
    const c = 3;
    const d = 4;
    const e = 5;
    return Effect.succeed(a + b + c + d + e);
});

export const functionExpressionPlainMany = function<T>(value: T) {
  const a = 1
  const b = 2
  const c = 3
  const d = 4
  const e = 5
  if (value === null) return Effect.fail("Error!")
  return Effect.succeed(a + b + c + d + e)
}

export function functionDeclarationPlainMany<T>(value: T) {
  const a = 1
  const b = 2
  const c = 3
  const d = 4
  const e = 5
  if (value === null) return Effect.fail("Error!")
  return Effect.succeed(a + b + c + d + e)
}


=== [D2] Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D2] Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D2] Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_plainMany.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// There should be two code fixes offered:
// 1. Effect.fn("inferredName") - uses the inferred span name, improves
//    tracing by adding spans. Recommended for app code.
// 2. Effect.fn (without name) - won't add tracing spans.
//
// Note: Known formatting divergences from upstream (TypeScript-Go printer artifacts):
// - Semicolons added to body statements inside replaced functions (e.g. `const a = 1;` vs `const a = 1`)
// - Space before parens in zero-parameter function expressions (`function ()` vs `function()`)
// - Trailing semicolons on replacement statements (`});` vs `})`)

export const arrowPlainMany = () => {
  const a = 1
  const b = 2
  const c = 3
  const d = 4
  const e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(a + b + c + d + e)
}

export const functionExpressionPlainMany = Effect.fn(function <T>(value: T) {
    const a = 1;
    const b = 2;
    const c = 3;
    const d = 4;
    const e = 5;
    if (value === null) return Effect.fail("Error!");
    return Effect.succeed(a + b + c + d + e);
});

export function functionDeclarationPlainMany<T>(value: T) {
  const a = 1
  const b = 2
  const c = 3
  const d = 4
  const e = 5
  if (value === null) return Effect.fail("Error!")
  return Effect.succeed(a + b + c + d + e)
}


=== [D2] Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fn(\"functionExpressionPlainMany\")" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_plainMany.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// There should be two code fixes offered:
// 1. Effect.fn("inferredName") - uses the inferred span name, improves
//    tracing by adding spans. Recommended for app code.
// 2. Effect.fn (without name) - won't add tracing spans.
//
// Note: Known formatting divergences from upstream (TypeScript-Go printer artifacts):
// - Semicolons added to body statements inside replaced functions (e.g. `const a = 1;` vs `const a = 1`)
// - Space before parens in zero-parameter function expressions (`function ()` vs `function()`)
// - Trailing semicolons on replacement statements (`});` vs `})`)

export const arrowPlainMany = () => {
  const a = 1
  const b = 2
  const c = 3
  const d = 4
  const e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(a + b + c + d + e)
}

export const functionExpressionPlainMany = Effect.fn("functionExpressionPlainMany")(function <T>(value: T) {
    const a = 1;
    const b = 2;
    const c = 3;
    const d = 4;
    const e = 5;
    if (value === null) return Effect.fail("Error!");
    return Effect.succeed(a + b + c + d + e);
});

export function functionDeclarationPlainMany<T>(value: T) {
  const a = 1
  const b = 2
  const c = 3
  const d = 4
  const e = 5
  if (value === null) return Effect.fail("Error!")
  return Effect.succeed(a + b + c + d + e)
}


=== [D3] Fix 0: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D3] Fix 1: "Disable effectFnOpportunity for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D3] Fix 2: "Convert to Effect.fn (no span)" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_plainMany.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// There should be two code fixes offered:
// 1. Effect.fn("inferredName") - uses the inferred span name, improves
//    tracing by adding spans. Recommended for app code.
// 2. Effect.fn (without name) - won't add tracing spans.
//
// Note: Known formatting divergences from upstream (TypeScript-Go printer artifacts):
// - Semicolons added to body statements inside replaced functions (e.g. `const a = 1;` vs `const a = 1`)
// - Space before parens in zero-parameter function expressions (`function ()` vs `function()`)
// - Trailing semicolons on replacement statements (`});` vs `})`)

export const arrowPlainMany = () => {
  const a = 1
  const b = 2
  const c = 3
  const d = 4
  const e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(a + b + c + d + e)
}

export const functionExpressionPlainMany = function<T>(value: T) {
  const a = 1
  const b = 2
  const c = 3
  const d = 4
  const e = 5
  if (value === null) return Effect.fail("Error!")
  return Effect.succeed(a + b + c + d + e)
}

export const functionDeclarationPlainMany = Effect.fn(function <T>(value: T) {
    const a = 1;
    const b = 2;
    const c = 3;
    const d = 4;
    const e = 5;
    if (value === null) return Effect.fail("Error!");
    return Effect.succeed(a + b + c + d + e);
});


=== [D3] Fix 3: "Convert to Effect.fn(\"functionDeclarationPlainMany\")" ===

--- file:///.src/effectFnOpportunity_plainMany.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

// There should be two code fixes offered:
// 1. Effect.fn("inferredName") - uses the inferred span name, improves
//    tracing by adding spans. Recommended for app code.
// 2. Effect.fn (without name) - won't add tracing spans.
//
// Note: Known formatting divergences from upstream (TypeScript-Go printer artifacts):
// - Semicolons added to body statements inside replaced functions (e.g. `const a = 1;` vs `const a = 1`)
// - Space before parens in zero-parameter function expressions (`function ()` vs `function()`)
// - Trailing semicolons on replacement statements (`});` vs `})`)

export const arrowPlainMany = () => {
  const a = 1
  const b = 2
  const c = 3
  const d = 4
  const e = 5
  return Effect.succeed(a + b + c + d + e)
}

export const functionExpressionPlainMany = function<T>(value: T) {
  const a = 1
  const b = 2
  const c = 3
  const d = 4
  const e = 5
  if (value === null) return Effect.fail("Error!")
  return Effect.succeed(a + b + c + d + e)
}

export const functionDeclarationPlainMany = Effect.fn("functionDeclarationPlainMany")(function <T>(value: T) {
    const a = 1;
    const b = 2;
    const c = 3;
    const d = 4;
    const e = 5;
    if (value === null) return Effect.fail("Error!");
    return Effect.succeed(a + b + c + d + e);
});
